description_long: This triumphal arch is a monumental stone structure featuring a single large central archway, flanked by two robust piers. The facade is richly adorned with sculptural reliefs, including circular medallions and intricate friezes, as well as two prominent statues standing on pedestals at the base of each pier. The upper section is capped with a projecting entablature and a decorative cornice, highlighted by a dark band that contrasts with the lighter stone below. Above the arch, a broad frieze panel appears suitable for inscriptions or commemorative text. The overall design is symmetrical and exudes a sense of grandeur, reminiscent of classical Roman or Neoclassical architecture.
description: A grand stone triumphal arch with sculptural reliefs, statues, and a prominent entablature, evoking classical architectural styles.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main archway, two statues at the base, decorative reliefs, and a broad inscription panel above.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the structure, the projecting entablature, and the profile of the statues.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the archway and decorative elements, but without statues or as much ornamentation as the front.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the structure's thickness and the side profile of the base.
